title: AEGIS Forecast Drift — Top Risk
description: Governed ranking and concentration view for forecast drift risk across forecast keys, regions, forecast versions, drift families and severity.

options.content:
# AEGIS Forecast Drift — Top Risk

Governed executive view of forecast drift risk — **V2** snapshot (read-only). Primary ranking metric is the governed `forecast_drift_score` (average per dimension); family rankings use the governed `forecast_drift_family_scores`. All panels respond to the shared filters above. Empty family scores are excluded, never treated as zero.

targets.filterExpression: region IN (${region:singlequote}, '__na__') && forecast_key IN (${forecast_key:singlequote}, '__na__') && fv_label IN (${forecast_version:singlequote}, '__na__') && drift_status IN (${drift_status:singlequote}, '__na__') && calculation_run_id IN (${run_id:singlequote}, '__na__')

panels.title: Top Drift Families by Average Family Score
panels.description: Source: forecast_drift_family_scores.csv (governed). Empty family scores excluded (never zero). This table lacks region / drift_status / run_id columns, so it honors Forecast Key and Forecast Version filters only.

panels.title: Drift Family Computability
panels.description: Source: forecast_drift_family_scores.csv. Avg / Max computed over computable (non-empty) scores only; Computable and Non-computable counts reconcile to the family total.

panels.title: Risk Concentration — Forecast Key × Drift Family (Avg Family Score)
panels.description: Decomposition of the governed per-signal family scores (performance / shape / stability / volatility) averaged per forecast key. Built from forecast_drift_signals.csv native columns — no join, honors all shared filters. Empty family scores excluded from the average.

panels.title: Consolidated Risk Details
panels.description: Per-signal governed detail from forecast_drift_signals.csv (one row per drift_event_id). Family scores are native columns, so no join is performed and the row count matches the signal universe. Sorted by forecast_drift_score descending.
